name: CULTURE Ontology (Strategic Culture Alignment)
description: Culture-strategy alignment assessment: four-quadrant alignment matrix (Aligned, Aspirational, Legacy, Conflicting), observable cultural signals (behaviours, incentives, rituals, symbols, language), culture shift targets, and quadrant-specific communication response patterns. Addresses the execution gap between stated strategy and observable organisational culture. Part of VE-Series VSOM-SC sub-series.

components.CultureStrategyAlignment: Container for a culture-strategy alignment assessment
components.AlignmentQuadrant: Classification of a strategic value in the alignment matrix: Aligned, Aspirational, Legacy, or Conflicting
components.CulturalSignal: Observable organisational signal: behaviours, incentives, rituals, symbols, or language
components.CultureShiftTarget: Measurable target for culture change linked to a strategic value
components.CommunicationResponse: Quadrant-specific communication approach for addressing culture-strategy gaps
components.CultureValue: A stated strategic value being assessed for cultural alignment
components.CultureAssessmentEntry: Individual value-to-signal mapping with quadrant classification

crossOntology.name: alignmentAssessesVSOM
crossOntology.purpose: Culture assessment evaluates alignment with VSOM strategy

crossOntology.name: signalObservedInContext
crossOntology.purpose: Cultural signals observed within organisational context

crossOntology.name: responseUsesPattern
crossOntology.purpose: Culture gap responses use narrative communication patterns

domain: Organisational Culture, Strategy Execution, Change Management

versionHistory.changes: Initial release — Culture-Strategy Alignment Matrix, Cultural Signals, Shift Targets, Communication Responses

notes: Layer C3 (Culture) of VSOM Strategy Communication. The most novel SC ontology — makes the gap between stated strategy and observable culture visible and measurable. Four quadrants define different communication responses to different alignment states.
